channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Deploying vLLM on OKE with NVIDIA A10 GPUs: The 20-Minute Setup Nobody Talks About
item.description: Last month I needed to stand up a Llama 3 inference endpoint for an internal tool. The requirements were simple: OpenAI-compatible API, auto-scaling, and it couldn't cost more than the team's...

item.title: Serving any LLM using a single command line with Flama
item.description: Flama 2.0 brings first-class support for generative AI: downloading, packaging, and serving large language models (LLMs) is now as simple as running a few commands in your terminal. No boilerplate...

item.title: Best LLM Models for Conversational AI in Language Learning
item.description: We are going to build a conversational language tutor that corrects mistakes in real time, adapts its complexity to your proficiency, and maintains context across a long practice session. It is...

item.title: Airtable AI From Scratch: A Freelance Dev's Cost Breakdown
item.description: Airtable AI From Scratch: A Freelance Dev's Cost Breakdown I run a one-person shop. No co-founders, no VC money, no "growth team." Just me, my laptop, and a growing list of clients who need AI...

item.title: Overcoming LLM Limitations
item.description: I recently shipped a small research agent that beats three recurring LLM problems: stale training data, hallucinated facts, and arithmetic errors. Instead of hoping the model memorized everything...

item.title: The Affective Blind Spot: How Support Systems Fail When Incentives Eclipse Care
item.description: When a support system breaks, its real incentives show. A field report on affective blindness, governance, and why bots escalate hostility but never harm. There is a moment in every system where...

item.title: Reducing LLM Costs: Best Practices and Techniques
item.description: LLM costs accumulate in ways that are not always obvious. Tokens consumed by system prompts, repeated context windows, and verbose JSON outputs all inflate bills before a single useful response is...

item.title: The Future of Large Language Models
item.description: We are building an autonomous research agent that turns a vague question into a structured plan, gathers evidence across multiple calls, and synthesizes a markdown report. This is the practical...

item.title: [ES] Cómo organizar el árbol de APIs REST para que sobreviva al tiempo (y a los cambios de organigrama)
item.description: También disponible en inglés Antes de diseñar un endpoint, clasificá su naturaleza. Recién después elegí su ubicación. El problema silencioso Existen muchos artículos que explican cómo escribir...

item.title: LLM Trends and Future Outlook
item.description: The conversation around large language models has shifted. The frontier is no longer defined solely by parameter counts or training compute, but by the economics and ergonomics of inference....

item.title: All the latest news on Android 17, Wear OS 7, and Android XR
item.description: Google’s Android 17 update includes highlights like new floating “Bubble” app windows for easier multitasking, a Screen Reaction recording mode, and a 50/50 split gaming mode for foldable phones....

item.title: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
item.description: Google has released Android 17 and Wear OS 7, introducing new multitasking features, parental controls, security tools, and smartwatch upgrades. The launch is also accompanied by a Pixel Drop that...

item.title: Android 17 arrives on Pixel phones today
item.description: Following its official debut last month, Google is now rolling out Android 17 to compatible Pixel phones, alongside additional exclusive features as part of the June Pixel Drop. Not every feature...

item.title: Google launches Wear OS 7 with Live Updates and a battery life boost
item.description: Google's Wear OS 7 update is starting to roll out today for the Pixel Watch 2, 3, and 4 , adding a new Live Updates feature that tracks live events from your Android smart watch. Live Updates will...

item.title: ‘Dangerous’ AI Models Are Coming No Matter What
item.description: The US government crackdown on Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 hides a glaring truth: AI models with advanced hacking capabilities will soon be the norm.

item.title: ClickFix Campaigns Expand Malware Delivery With New Loaders and Fake Update Lures
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged multiple ClickFix campaigns that deliver three malware loaders called BabaDeda Loader, Lorem Ipsum Loader, and Potemkin, per independent reports from...

item.title: Verizon’s ‘Simplicity’ flat-rate plan starts at $30 per month for new customers
item.description: Verizon is launching a new Simplicity plan that starts at $30 / month for new customers, or $45 / month for existing ones. In its announcement, Verizon says the plan drops activation and upgrade...

item.title: Apple 2027 rumors: AirPods with cameras for AI and the second folding iPhone
item.description: Now that we're clear of WWDC and all of the new AI-powered features coming to Apple's platforms, Bloomberg reporter Mark Gurman has more details about rumored new hardware, like the...

item.title: Qualcomm’s latest chip hints that more powerful smart glasses could be on the way
item.description: Smart glasses are still a nascent category, but chipmaker Qualcomm is hard at work upgrading the silicon to power the next wave of XR devices: the Snapdragon Reality Elite. Although Qualcomm is...

item.title: The Google / Xreal Aura XR glasses are now available to preorder
item.description: The Project Aura glasses collaboration between Xreal and Google is now one step closer to being something you can buy. Reservations for the second Android XR device, now dubbed the Xreal Aura, are...

item.title: Snap is finally about to ship AR glasses — and they cost a fortune
item.description: Snap is finally launching augmented glasses for the public. Specs, which Snap describes as "a wearable computer built into see-through augmented reality glasses," will cost $2,195. You can...

item.title: Sixty percent of US consumers say ‘AI’ in brand messaging is a turnoff, survey finds
item.description: WordPress VIP’s latest survey suggests consumers are wary of AI-generated answers even as companies increasingly view AI search as an important referral channel.

item.title: Everything you need to know about Prime Day 2026
item.description: Amazon Prime Day 2026 is getting closer, and it’s possible you might have some questions about the sale. When does it start? Why is it in June instead of July? And, most importantly, when will the...

item.title: Christopher Golda and Grey Baker Join YC as General Partners
item.description: We're excited to announce that Christopher Golda and Grey Baker are joining YC as General Partners.

item.title: Nothing’s Ear (a), our favorite budget earbuds, are cheaper than ever ahead of Prime Day
item.description: If you’re looking for a cheap Father’s Day gift that delivers more than you’d expect for the price, Nothing’s Ear (a) are a good option. The wireless earbuds are currently on sale as part of a...

item.title: SpaceX is public: Everything you need to know post-IPO
item.description: TechCrunch has followed SpaceX's start, struggles, and successes from the early days. And we're here for what happens next too. This package of SpaceX IPO coverage includes who stands to win (and...

item.title: DOJ claims xAI’s unpermitted gas turbines are a matter of ‘national, economic, and energy security’
item.description: The Justice department says the Pentagon needs xAI to keep using its unpermitted gas turbines.

item.title: Plaud says its software business topped $100M in ARR after shipping over 2M AI notetakers
item.description: Plaud is trying to make a mark in a crowded market full of AI-powered meeting notetakers.

item.title: Robinhood’s note on 10% layoffs shows blaming AI isn’t cutting it
item.description: Unlike many of his tech industry peers who have cut thousands of jobs citing the need to restructure to make the most of AI, Robinhood's CEO Vlad Tenev conspicuously made no mention of AI in his...

item.title: My Father Wants to Age in Place. AI Will Be Watching
item.description: Devices that monitor seniors for safety are appealing to worried loved ones and underresourced home care agencies.
